questions.question: Qutb-ud-din Aibak was given the title __________ because of his generosity.
The word 'Mamluk' means __________ in Arabic.
Iltutmish shifted the capital of the Delhi Sultanate from __________ to Delhi.
The __________ system introduced by Iltutmish involved land assignments to officers with military responsibility.
In 1221, Iltutmish refused shelter to the ruler of __________ who was being chased by Genghis Khan.
__________ was the first and last Muslim woman to rule over Delhi.
Balban propagated the __________ theory to establish the supreme authority of the Sultan.
Balban created the __________ department for military affairs.
Ala-ud-din Khalji called himself the deputy of the __________.
__________ led the southern expeditions during Ala-ud-din Khalji's rule.
Ala-ud-din Khalji abolished small iqtas and converted them into Crown lands called __________.
The superintendent of the market during Ala-ud-din Khalji's time was called __________.
Ala-ud-din Khalji built __________ Fort in 1302.
__________ was the court poet during Ala-ud-din Khalji's reign.
Ghazi Malik ascended the throne under the title of __________.
Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq renamed Devagiri as __________.
Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq introduced __________ as token currency in place of silver tanka.
Firoz Shah Tughlaq established the __________ for the welfare of the poor.
Firoz Shah Tughlaq built canals from the __________ to Hissar for irrigation.
__________ invaded India in 1398, leaving the Sultanate crumbling.
Khizr Khan, the founder of the Sayyid Dynasty, took the title __________.
The last Sayyid ruler was __________.
Sikandar Lodi's real name was __________.
Sikandar Lodi moved the capital from Delhi to __________.
Babur defeated __________ in the Battle of Panipat in 1526, ending the Delhi Sultanate.
